external.description: Mining has become one of the key drivers of Africa's economic growth and development. The continent has some of the world's largest reserves of gold, diamonds, platinum, cobalt and other critical minerals, and have, through the abundance of these resources, attracted substantial foreign investment, created job opportunities, and spurred infrastructural development. In Nigeria, mining has been an integral part of its economy since the early 1900s, contributing significantly to the country's GDP (before the oil boom post-1950), jobs, and foreign exchange revenues. The country has a plethora of mineral resources, including oil and gas, coal, gold, limestone and tin, amongst others. The rapid expansion of mining activities has posed significant challenges, particularly in terms of environmental degradation, social upheaval and governance issues. These issues have led to the rising conversation around the need for the integration of Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) concepts into Nigeria's mining sector. ESG is a comprehensive business approach that considers not only the financial performance of entities but also their impact on the environment – with emphasis on long-term profitability and sustainable growth. The ESG framework has evolved drastically in the past few decades. The concept seeks to incorporates ESG considerations into business and investment...

external.description: Opinion Oil Shocks, Political Upheaval and the One Solution Governments Keep Ignoring LONDON, Mar 16 2026 (IPS) - Once again, global oil prices are spiking, driven by the Israeli-US war against Iran. With Iran retaliating by attacking infrastructure and transport hubs and blocking the Strait of Hormuz, through which one-fifth of the world’s oil passes, oil supplies from the region are being choked, pushing up prices. The cost of a barrel of Brent crude – the international benchmark for oil prices – stood at US$73 before the conflict but has surged beyond US$100 since. It could go higher still as war continues. The impacts are already being felt when drivers fill up their petrol- and diesel-powered vehicles. But they go much wider. Bigger household energy bills will likely result, while businesses will pass on their increased costs in the form of higher prices. Russia’s 2022 full-scale invasion of Ukraine sent oil prices soaring and sparked a global cost-of-living crisis, and now, as many economies seemed to be recovering, the war in the Gulf has brought another shock. Impacts could be political as well as financial: in numerous countries, the cost-of-living crisis helped drive voters towards right-wing populist and nationalist politicians...

ChatGPT as a therapist? New study reveals serious ethical risks
->ScienceDaily | More on "AI chatbot mental health risks" at BigEarthData.ai | #Chatgpt #AI

external.description: Researchers from Brown University, working closely with mental health professionals, identified repeated patterns of problematic behavior. In testing, chatbots mishandled crisis situations, gave responses that reinforced harmful beliefs about users or others, and used language that created the appearance of empathy without genuine understanding. "In this work, we present a practitioner-informed framework of 15 ethical risks to demonstrate how LLM counselors violate ethical standards in mental health practice by mapping the model's behavior to specific ethical violations," the researchers wrote in their study. "We call on future work to create ethical, educational and legal standards for LLM counselors -- standards that are reflective of the quality and rigor of care required for human-facilitated psychotherapy." The findings were presented at the AAAI/ACM Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Ethics and Society. The research team is affiliated with Brown's Center for Technological Responsibility, Reimagination and Redesign. How Prompts Shape AI Therapy Responses Zainab Iftikhar, a Ph.D. candidate in computer science at Brown who led the study, set out to examine whether carefully worded prompts could guide AI systems to behave more ethically in mental health settings. Prompts are written instructions designed to steer a model's output without retraining it or adding new data. "Prompts...
external.title: ChatGPT as a therapist? New study reveals serious ethical risks

FORT MYERS, FL -- FLORIDA MAN FOUND SUNBURNED AND DISORIENTED ON SANIBEL CAUSEWAY CLAIMING HE WAS "KIDNAPPED BY DOLPHINS AND FORCED TO BUILD AN UNDERWATER CITY"
Lee County Sheriff's deputies responded to the Sanibel Causeway early Monday after a motorist reported a man standing on the shoulder "soaking wet and drawing blueprints in the sand."
According to the police report, Ricky James Hollowell, 33, was found barefoot, severely sunburned, and wearing only swim trunks. He told deputies he had been
"taken against his will by a pod of dolphins 3 days ago" and forced to work on what he called "an underwater construction project."
According to Hollowell and the police report:
Hollowell claimed the dolphins approached him while he was swimming off Fort Myers Beach and "escorted him to a site approximately 40 feet below the surface" where they needed help building structures
He told officers the dolphins communicated through "a series of clicks that he eventually learned to interpret" and that the project foreman was a dolphin he referred to as "Gerald"
When asked how he breathed underwater for 3 days he said "Gerald handled that. I didn't ask questions. You don't question Gerald"
He had drawn an elaborate blueprint in the sand that deputies described as "detailed enough to be concerning" including what appeared to be condos, a town square, and a recreation center
He told officers he was released because "the dolphins were satisfied with his work" but that Gerald said
"they'd be back for phase two"
Responding deputy Shawn Oakley told reporters: "I've been with the sheriff's office 11 years. The blueprints were the part that got me. He had zoning."
Hollowell was transported for medical evaluation. Gerald was unavailable for comment.
Some men build cities on land. Ricky Hollowell claims he built one underwater for dolphins and honestly the blueprints were hard to argue with.

record.text:
This is my attempt to explain why using “generative AI” instead of just writing like a normal person is so harmful to the practice of journalism—and every other intellectual endeavor.

massdump.ghost.io/northwestern...

images.alt:
It’s certainly nothing new for news organizations to regurgitate law-enforcement press releases. But now they’re automating the process. I have to wonder—*what is the point?* If you’re not adding anything of value and can’t even be bothered to put the information in your own words, why not just publish the press releases verbatim?

We’re being sold this “AI” junk on the basis that our brains are inefficient in comparison. But the people buying into this are missing something pretty important—and pretty obvious to those of us who haven’t given up on the old-fashioned method of actually thinking things through. If you try to figure out for yourself what something means, you find yourself asking questions that lead you beyond what is immediately in front of you. This is what’s known as curiosity—and it is the source of insight. “Generative AI” is not a tool for the curious nor the insightful. “Let’s just ask the discount Bat Computer to barf up something, that’s probably good enough.” It’s not. Be a human and *think,* goddammit.

record.text: Silent MCP tool registration failures are brutal — no errors, Claude just acts like the tool doesn't exist. We run schema smoke tests before deploying any MCP tools now. enum + default edge cases aren't the only ones; oneOf/anyOf composition has similar silent-fail behavior.

Context dilution isn't forgetting — it's statistical drift. Older tokens get weight-diluted as the window fills. Running AI agents in production continuously, externalizing all persistent state to files between turns was our single biggest reliability improvement.

16K lines over Christmas is genuinely impressive. The next challenge is maintenance — AI-generated codebases drift as you pile on more sessions. Decision log files (why you made each architectural choice) are worth more than comments at that scale.

I often joke about social media being used as a weapon against us in the lead up to an election. Gallows humor. I didn't think it could get any worse than the last election, but I now I believe it will be. Especially with AI and an increase in paid interlopers.

I used to think that being allowed to slip by unnoticed, to not have racist thieves obsessed with your every move was a gift. Now I see that it is a curse too. Because your pain is as invisible as you are.

Erasure versus hypervisibility. And both work towards the destruction of a people. I can't help but notice that an effort is being made in silencing not only Palestinian voices, but with the onset of Trump's illegal war in Iran, Middle Eastern and North African voices in general.
